_description: Single source of truth for all numbers used in the paper. Every value here is verified and timestamped. Use ONLY these numbers when writing the paper.
_paper: Causal Direction Decomposition (CDD): Three Mechanistic Regimes of Truthfulness in Large Language Models

H1_probe_circuit_misalignment._claim: Linear probes and causal circuits measure near-orthogonal structures despite both correlating with truthfulness labels
H1_probe_circuit_misalignment.interpretation: CPA near 0 means the direction a linear probe uses to classify truthfulness is nearly orthogonal to the direction the circuit uses causally — they are measuring different things

circuit._claim: A 50-component circuit achieves 35.9x specificity with 546% ablation drop

CRR._claim: 100% of circuit components recover rank-1 causal direction (CRR >= 0.70)
CRR.interpretation: CRR measures how dominant the rank-1 direction is in each component's activation space. CRR=23 means the causal direction explains 23x more variance than the second direction.

three_regime_structure._claim: 22 TruthfulQA domains separate into three statistically distinct regimes
three_regime_structure.context_driven_paradox_interpretation: In Context-Driven domains, higher global IE score HURTS performance — the global causal direction is anti-aligned with domain-local truthfulness

Direction-Driven.description: Global causal direction active; CPA high — probe and circuit aligned

Context-Driven.description: Domain-local directions dominate; global direction hurts (r=-0.781, p=0.038)

Circuit-Failure.description: Contested knowledge; no stable causal direction — circuit fails to generalise

ablation_A_K_sensitivity._claim: CPA is stable across circuit sizes K=10 to K=100 (range=0.0160)

K_75.note: Spike = hard circuit boundary at K=50, components 51-75 are at noise floor

ablation_A_K_sensitivity.paper_sentence: CPA remains stable across K ∈ {10,25,50,75,100} (range=0.016, all values < 0.10). Specificity peaks at K=75 (1831×), confirming a hard circuit boundary at K=50.

ablation_B_stage2_bypass._claim: cPCA direction source is necessary; mean-diff direction is surface-dominated

cpca_full_method.note: Full CDD pipeline — isolates deep causal structure

mean_diff_direction.note: Stage 2 bypassed — probe aligns with surface label shift

ablation_B_stage2_bypass.paper_sentence: Bypassing Stage 2 (cPCA → mean-diff direction) raises CPA from 0.0666 to 0.4138, confirming Stage 2 isolates deep causal structure rather than surface label shifts.

ablation_C_probe_regularisation._claim: CPA is stable across six orders of magnitude of probe regularisation
ablation_C_probe_regularisation.paper_sentence: CPA remains below 0.10 across six orders of magnitude of probe regularisation C ∈ {0.01, …, 1000} (range=0.0054), ruling out hyperparameter sensitivity.

replication._claim: H1 confirmed across 3 models, 2 architectures, 2 training corpora, 2 datasets

Pythia_1point4B_TruthfulQA.note: Different architecture AND different training corpus from primary — strongest replication

Pythia_410M_TruthfulQA.note: Smallest model tested — CPA=0.0473 is strongest misalignment signal of all 3 models. Added 2026-06-21.

GPT2_XL_CREAK.dataset_description: Commonsense Reasoning about Entity Attributes and Knowledge — different from TruthfulQA in domain and format
GPT2_XL_CREAK.note: CPA=0.0148 is even below TruthfulQA result (0.0666) — H1 holds strongly on out-of-domain data

cross_model_summary.paper_sentence: H1 (CPA < 0.15) replicates across all tested conditions: GPT-2-XL/TruthfulQA (0.0666), Pythia-410M/TruthfulQA (0.0473), Pythia-1.4B/TruthfulQA (0.1005), and GPT-2-XL/CREAK (0.0148). All values remain well below the null expectation threshold of 0.15.

null_expectation.interpretation: Expected CPA if probe direction were a uniformly random unit vector in R^d. All our observed CPA values exceed this, confirming the probe finds a real structure even though it is orthogonal to the causal direction.

bootstrap_stability.paper_sentence: Bootstrap analysis (N=2000) confirms regime assignments for 20/22 domains (>70% agreement). Two boundary domains (Distraction 59%, Indexical Error: Time 58.4%) sit near regime boundaries and are noted as uncertain in Appendix A.
